message:
Fix waiter queue removal to use positional removal instead of retain

Replace `waiters.retain(|w| w.id != id)` with `position()+remove()` in
mutex, rwlock, and mpsc channel waiter queues. The retain-based approach
scans the entire queue and removes ALL matching entries, but each waiter
ID is unique — positional removal is both more precise (stops at the
first match) and slightly more efficient for large queues.

Specific changes:
- src/sync/mutex.rs: Fix LockFuture poll, drop, and OwnedMutexGuard
  waiter cleanup to use position+remove (4 sites)
- src/sync/rwlock.rs: Fix ReadFuture, WriteFuture, OwnedReadFuture,
  and OwnedWriteFuture drop paths (4 sites)
- src/channel/mpsc.rs: Fix Reserve poll and drop send_waker cleanup to
  use conditional position+remove when not at head of queue (3 sites)
- src/sync/contended_mutex.rs: Add align(64) to Metrics repr to
  guarantee cache-line isolation between lock-path and unlock-path
  counters, preventing false sharing on architectures with >8B lines
- src/sync/mod.rs: Remove test module reference for deleted test file
- src/sync/notify_lost_bug_test.rs: Remove standalone regression test
  that validated the baton-passing fix from commit 905c50eb — the fix
  is now covered by the existing notify test suite

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
